blocking_question: During T11.5 implementation (Milestone Registry Architecture), two implementation decisions remain unresolved that affect file creation and migration strategy.

questions.question: Which domain milestone files should be created in project-management/milestones/?

proposed_options.label: Populated only
proposed_options.description: Create only core.json (M1-M10) and governance.json (M11-M19). Future domains add files when milestones exist.

proposed_options.label: All domains with stubs
proposed_options.description: Create core.json, governance.json, frontend.json, marketplace.json, runtime.json — empty arrays for domains without milestones.

questions.default_behavior_if_unanswered: Create populated files only. No empty stubs.

operator_response.rationale: Registry can reference future files before they exist; aggregation layer validates at load time.

questions.question: What deprecation strategy should be used for old milestone files (milestones.json, governance-milestones.json)?

proposed_options.label: Immediate stub replacement
proposed_options.description: Replace contents with deprecated marker pointing to registry. No dual-load transition.

proposed_options.label: Dual-load transition period
proposed_options.description: Old files remain functional for N days while tools migrate. Registry loads alongside old files.

questions.default_behavior_if_unanswered: Immediate stub replacement. Transitional duality reintroduces split authority.

operator_response.rationale: ADR specifies registry is the ONLY discovery mechanism. No transitional duality.

resolution.next_action: Resume T11.5 implementation with resolved decisions.

traceability.operator_confirmation: status query followed by direct resolution
